english: to become, will, to be done (passive voice)
must, to have to, to be obliged (to do something)
french: devoir, être obligé (de faire quelque chose)

english: shall, to be asked to (do something), to be supposed to (do something)
french: devoir, être invité (à faire quelque chose)

english: to be allowed (to do something), may
french: être autorisé (à faire quelque chose), peut

english: to make, to prepare, to do
to do, to do something to someone
french: faire, faire quelque chose à quelqu'un

english: to go, to leave, to work, to function (coll.), to be going (Dat.), to be all right (Dat.)
to come, to make it (e.g. over something), to go to, to occur
to bring, to take, to lead
to stand, to be, to appear
to take, to cause oneself to be (in some state)
to receive, to catch (e.g. a cold), to sit well with someone
french: recevoir, attraper (par exemple un rhume), s'entendre avec quelqu'un

english: to forget, to leave (forget to take)
to hold, to stop (a movement), to support, to keep
to wait, to maintain (e.g. a car)
to know, to be acquainted with, to be familiar with
to understand, to get along with someone
to offer, to bid, to arise, to occur (of an opportunity) (refl.)
french: offrir, enchérir, surgir, se produire (d'une opportunité) (réfl.)

english: to get dressed, to put on, to dress someone, to attract, to pull
french: s'habiller, mettre, habiller quelqu'un, attirer, tirer

english: to take something off, to undress, to pull out, to move out
french: enlever quelque chose, se déshabiller, se retirer, sortir

english: to go (by vehicle), to leave, to drive
to meet (refl.), to encounter (refl.), to hit, to affect
to learn, to study, to train something
french: apprendre, étudier, se former à quelque chose

english: to show, to point at, to demonstrate
to walk, to flow (of a fluid), to run (of an event)
french: marcher, couler (un fluide), se dérouler (un événement)

english: to explain, to declare, to assert
to pay (for something), to atone for
to cook, to boil (to heat a liquid), to boil something
french: cuire, faire bouillir (chauffer un liquide), faire bouillir quelque chose

english: to live, to reside, to dwell
to wear (clothing), to carry, to hold
to interest, to be interested in (refl.)
to pull, to drag, to move
to happen (colloquial), to move beyond, to strain
to call by telephone, to call on (someone)
to stop, to cease an activity or process
french: arrêter, cesser une activité ou un processus

english: to begin (on a certain date or time)
french: commencer (à une certaine date ou heure)

english: to be seated, to stay, to fit (of clothing)
to get up, to be open (e.g. a window, door)
french: se lever, être ouvert (par exemple une fenêtre, une porte)

english: to position, to put, to catch
to lay, to lean, to lie down
to make glad, to be glad (refl.), to look forward (refl.)
french: rendre heureux, être heureux (réfl.), attendre avec impatience (réfl.)

english: to shut, to close, to lock, to conclude
to invite, to load (something into a container)
french: inviter, charger (quelque chose dans un conteneur)
